The Schedule is provided for a convenience and a general guide; it is not exhaustive in listing every charge that may arise during the life of your loan. The absence of a fee in the Schedule does not mean that the fee cannot be charged. In addition to the fees below, we may charge you for applicable and incurred third-party expenses, where permitted by applicable law.

2. RETURNED PAYMENT CHARGES

If any payment, for any reason, is rejected or returned (including, without limitation, checks or electronic funds transfers), the Borrower will be charged an insufficient funds or returned item fee in the maximum amount allowed by law. In such cases, we may assess a charge regardless of whether the payment was made as scheduled, and regardless of whether you are individually benefited by the returned or rejected payment. A returned item or rejected transaction will not be considered a cure for any missed on-time payment obligation that is due at the time it is returned or rejected.

3. LATE CHARGES

Where applicable, this charge will be assessed for payments that are not made within a specified grace period, if one is provided. If the Loan is not paid within this grace period, we may assess a late charge in the maximum amount allowed by law for the entire amount of the payment due up until it is paid. The late charge will remain in effect until the outstanding amount is collected in full.

BINDING ARBITRATION AGREEMENT AND WAIVER OF JURY TRIAL – LOANDEPOT.COM, LLC  
Binding Arbitration Agreement  

This Binding Arbitration Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into between Heather Jimenez (“you” or “Borrower”) and loanDepot.com, LLC (“we,” “us,” or “Lender”) in connection with the loan identified as loan number 1939168097 and any account, transaction, or relationship arising therefrom. By signing the related loan documents or otherwise accepting the terms set forth herein, you acknowledge that you have read this Agreement, that you understand it materially affects your legal rights, and that you agree to resolve Covered Disputes through individual binding arbitration rather than in court before a judge or jury. Please read this Agreement carefully and retain a copy for your records. Capitalized terms not defined herein have the meanings given in the loan documents.

1. AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE  
You and loanDepot.com, LLC agree that, except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement, any Covered Dispute (as defined below) shall be resolved by arbitration, which is a private dispute resolution process instead of a court, in any form of class, collective, representative, or private attorney general action. You and Lender acknowledge that the mutual intention of the parties is to provide a fair, efficient, and cost-effective resolution of disputes and therefore, each party waives their right to participate in any type of class action and agrees to arbitrate Covered Disputes on an individual basis.

This Agreement is governed by the Federal Arbitration Act, 9 U.S.C. Section 1 et seq. (the "FAA"), and shall be interpreted broadly in favor of arbitration. To the extent the FAA is found not to apply to this Agreement, or if such an interpretation is held to be invalid, then the laws of the state in which the Loan was originated shall govern this Agreement. You and loanDepot.com, LLC agree that any dispute arising under this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the state of California, without regard to its conflict of law principles.
  
2. DISPUTES COVERED  
A “Covered Dispute” means any claim, dispute, or controversy of any kind, whether based in contract, tort, statute, regulation, common law, equity, or otherwise, that arises out of or relates in any way to the

loan, the loan documents, this Agreement, the property securing the loan, the origination, underwriting, processing, closing, funding, servicing, collection, or enforcement of the loan, or any communication, disclosure, enforcement, product, or service associated with the relationship between you and loanDepot.com, LLC. The term is to be construed in the broadest manner permitted by law. Covered Disputes also include, without limitation, claims relating to the validity, enforceability, scope, or interpretation of this Agreement, except as expressly reserved for judicial determination; claims regarding alleged fraud, misrepresentation, breach of fiduciary duty, or unfair deceptive acts or practices; claims under federal or state consumer-protection, lending, privacy, or fair-credit-reporting statutes; and claims arising from any prior loan, account, or transaction between you and the Lender. Claims by or against the Lender's affiliates, agents, employees, officers, directors, successors, assigns, and servicers are likewise included. The fact that a Covered Dispute may also constitute a claim by or against a third party shall not defeat the obligation to arbitrate as between you and loanDepot.com, LLC. if any portion of a dispute falls within the scope of this Agreement and any portion does not, the parties shall arbitrate the portion that is covered and may seek my the remaining portion of the dispute to the extent permitted by applicable law and the rules of the administering body. 3. DISPUTES EXCLUDED Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement does not require arbitration of the following matters: any action by loanDepot.com, LLC to foreclose upon, or to exercise any right related to, securing the loan through judicial or non-judicial foreclosure, power of sale, and executed in lieu of, either parties' right to seek a court-ordered injunction or other relief such as available at law or in equity has not been excluded by the Agreement and shall not be subject to this arbitration, nor the filing of encumbrance of any lien against the collateral. The Lender's pursuit of such remedies will not be instituted until after you have initiated any covered DISPUTE. This Agreement also does not prohibit any party from seeking provider-specific remedies usually constructed under relevant laws, interpretations, actions, and remedies, whether interim or long-standing or resolutions until after the effects of or proceeding in relation to mediation, arbitration or settlement relating to such obligation to arbitrate. Nothing in this Agreement limits your right to file a complaint with, or to provide information to, any federal, state or local regulatory agency or regulatory authority, including any consumer-protection agency, regarding any issues or concerns related to consumer-protection laws, nor does it prevent you from pursuing such applicable law or reverse remedies by not agreeing to pre-dispute binding arbitration 4. SMALL-CLAIMS CARVE-OUT Either you or loanDepot.com, LLC may elect to bring and pursue an individual claim in a small-claims court (or other court of limited jurisdiction) in lieu of arbitration, provided that the claim qualifies for that court under its jurisdictional limits and remains in that forum on an individual basis. This carve-out is intended to

preserve a simple, low-cost venue for the resolution of modest individual disputes without the procedures associated with formal arbitration or litigation. If a claim brought in small-claims court is transferred, removed, appealed to, or otherwise proceeded in a court other than a small-claims court, or if the claim is amended to seek relief or to assert allegations that exceed the jurisdiction of the small-claims court, then either party may thereafter elect to compel arbitration that claim under this Agreement. Any such election shall be honored by the parties, and the dispute shall proceed in arbitration in accordance with the terms set forth herein. A claim brought in small-claims court must be asserted only in the claimant's individual capacity and may not be maintained as, or consolidated with, any class, collectively, or representative action. The class-action waiver and other individual-relief provisions of this Agreement apply with equal force to any matter pursued under this small-claims carve-out.
5. ADMINISTERING BODY AND RULES Unless the parties agree in writing to a different forum, a Covered Dispute submitted to arbitration shall be administered by a nationally recognized, neutral arbitration administrator that maintains established procedures for the resolution of consumer disputes, in accordance with that administrator's consumer arbitration rules. In the event that the administrator or arbitration service chosen by the parties in a single neutral arbitrator is not a retired judge or an attorney experienced in consumer finance or real-property matters, then the parties shall select again from a list of arbitrators provided by the arbitration administrator. To the extent the chosen administrator's rules conflict with the terms of this Agreement, the terms of this Agreement shall prevail. In the event the parties do not agree on the arbitration administrator, any party may apply to a court of competent jurisdiction to appoint an arbitration administrator. The arbitrator shall apply applicable substantive law, conform to the forms and limitations of this Agreement and shall have the authority to determine the arbitrability of any Covered Dispute and to resolve any issues regarding the scope, applicability, enforceability of this Agreement, except that the arbitrator may not determine whether the Class-Action Waiver in this Agreement is enforceable. The arbitrator shall honor claims of privilege and confidentiality and may issue appropriate protective orders to protect those claims. You may contact the administration directly at info@loaned.com, LLC at 866.511.3734/8767 or by writing to 138 Volker Circle Suite 720, New Austin, MN 46400.
6. EFFECT OF ARBITRATION; JURY-TRIAL WAIVER BY AGREEING TO ARBITRATE COVERED DISPUTES, YOU AND LOANED.COM, LLC EACH KNOWINGLY, VOLUNTARILY, AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVE THE RIGHT TO A TRIAL BY JURY AND THE RIGHT TO HAVE COVERED DISPUTES DECIDED BY A JUDGE IN A COURT OF LAW. Arbitration substitutes a streamlined procedure for the procedures available in court. The parties understand that such waiver is a material inducement to enter into this Agreement and that such waiver allows the opportunity to consult with legal counsel of its own choosing regarding the consequences of this waiver.

In arbitration, the proceedings are generally more streamlined than litigation, discovery may be more limited, and the rights to pre-trial and appellate review are substantially narrower than those available in court. The arbitrator's decision is final and binding, subject to the limited grounds for review provided under the FAA. Judgment on the award rendered by the arbitrator may be enforced in any court of competent jurisdiction. The parties accept these features of arbitration in exchange for its efficiency, finality, and reduced costs.

The arbitrator may award any relief, including monetary damages, declaratory relief, injunctive relief, and statutory remedies, that a court could award to an individual party under applicable law, and only in favor of the individual party seeking relief and only to the extent necessary to provide relief warranted by that party's individual claim. The arbitrator shall also reasoned award setting forth the essential findings and conclusions on which the award is based, in accordance with the administrator's rules.

7. CLASS-ACTION AND REPRESENTATIVE WAIVER
COVERED DISPUTES SHALL BE ARBITRATED ONLY ON AN INDIVIDUAL BASIS. YOU AND LOANDEPOT.COM, LLC EACH WAIVE ANY RIGHT TO ARBITRATE OR LITIGATE ANY COVERED DISPUTE AS A CLASS ACTION, COLLECTIVE ACTION, PRIVATE ATTORNEY-GENERAL ACTION, OR OTHER REPRESENTATIVE PROCEEDING, OR TO SERVE AS A CLASS REPRESENTATIVE OR CLASS MEMBER, OR TO PARTICIPATE AS A MEMBER OF A CLASS OF CLAIMANTS. The arbitrator shall have no authority to consolidate or join the claims of more than one person and may not otherwise permit any form of class, collective, or representative proceeding.

The parties agree that this class-action and representative waiver is a fundamental and non-severable provision of this Agreement. In the event that this class-action and representative waiver is found to be unenforceable or invalid as to a particular Covered Dispute or claim for relief, then, to that extent, that Covered Dispute or claim shall be decided in a court of competent jurisdiction. All other Covered Disputes and claims shall remain subject to arbitration on an individual basis pursuant to this Agreement.

8. COSTS AND FEES
Each party shall bear its own costs and any fee filing fee incurred in the administration; except that, upon a party’s request, the arbitrator shall determine and award to the prevailing party its reasonable attorneys’ fees, expert fees, and other costs incurred in arbitration, unless the statute under which the claim is brought provides otherwise. The arbitrator may not prohibit recovery of the costs of pursuing the claim.

Each party shall submit a statement reflecting the fees, expenses, and other costs incurred in the arbitration proceeding to the arbitrator, who will allocate the reasonable costs to the prevailing party to the extent such award would be available under the applicable substantive law.

governing the Covered Dispute, or as otherwise provided by the loan documents. The allocation of fees and costs that be set forth in the arbitrator's award and is subject to the same frinality as the remainder of the award. If the arbitrator determines that a claim or defense was frivolous, asserted in bad faith, or brought for an improper purpose, the arbitrator may reallocate the fees and costs of the arbitration as authorized by the administrator's rules and applicable law. Nothing in this section limits any statutory right you may have to recover fees and costs that you would be entitled to have the Covered Dispute been adjudicated in court. 1. We recommend that you retain proof of mailing your opt-out notice for your records and that you contact us at 686.511.3734x7487 if you have questions regarding this right.

II. SEVERABILITY AND SURVIVAL
Except as otherwise provided with respect to the class-action and representative waiver in Section 7, if any portion of this Agreement is found to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court or arbitrator of competent jurisdiction, that portion shall be severed and the remainder of this Agreement shall be construed and enforced to the fullest extent permitted by law as if the severed portion had not been included. The invalidity of any provision in one jurisdiction shall not affect the validity or enforceability of that provision in any other jurisdiction.

This Agreement shall survive the repayment, prepayment, refinancing, modification, satisfaction, charge-off, or cancellation of the loan; the sale, assignment, or transfer of the loan or its servicing to any successor, assignee, or servicer; the closing or termination of any account or relationship between you and loanDepot.com, LLC; and the bankruptcy, insolvency, or dissolution of either party to the maximum extent permitted by applicable law. The provisions of this Agreement inure to the benefit of and are binding upon the parties and their respective heirs, successors, and permitted successors.

This Agreement, together with the arbitration-related provisions of the loan documents, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties regarding the arbitration of Covered Disputes. Any amendments to this Agreement shall be effective unless made in writing and signed by both parties. By proceeding with the loan transaction identified by loan number 19319960027, dated February 05, 2024, you acknowledge your understanding of and agreement to the terms set forth herein.
